stories.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
stories.description: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

stories.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
stories.description: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks

stories.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
stories.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

stories.title: Kushner and U.S. envoy Witkoff will head to Pakistan for new Iran talks
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire. And, Trump eases medical marijuana rules
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend their ceasefire for three weeks, President Trump says. And, the Trump administration is easing rules on medical marijuana.

stories.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.

stories.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.description: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.title: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.description: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.title: US-Kuwaiti journalist held in Kuwait over social media posts acquitted, lawyers say
stories.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin reportedly faced charges, including spreading false information, after he re-posted images related to the Iran war.

stories.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz cannot be opened due to ceasefire breaches
stories.description: Iran's chief negotiator says "violations" by the US and Israel make it impossible to open the strait.

stories.title: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.description: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.title: Tracking ships under fire in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran's top negotiator says it's "not possible to reopen" the strait due to "blatent violations" of US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.title: US and Iran 'locked in war of words' says Lyse Doucet
stories.description: The US confirmed on Tuesday night that the Vice-President's trip to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks has been cancelled.

stories.title: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.description: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.title: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.description: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Lebanon despite ceasefire extension
stories.description: Lebanese state media reported a series of new Israeli strikes in at least four different locations in the country's south late Saturday, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered his military to attack Hezbollah following alleged ceasefire breaches. Attacks earlier ...

stories.title: US-Iran talks in doubt after Trump scraps Pakistan visit
stories.description: International efforts to mediate between the United States and Iran were in the balance on Sunday, after President Donald Trump canceled his envoys' planned trip to Pakistan for talks. Trump said there was no point "sitting around talking about nothing" and dismissed Tehran's neg...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran's FM to return to Pakistan for talks as US envoys cancel visit
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is expected to return to Pakistan on Sunday for more peace negotiations, state media reported, having just left Islamabad the previous day. US envoys did not arrive in Islamabad as expected on Saturday after Iran said it would not participa...

stories.title: Strait talks: What's at stake in Hormuz
stories.description: The Strait of Hormuz, which is a key passage for oil produced in the Middle East, has come to the center of the Iran-US conflict.

stories.title: Israel destroys solar panels in south Lebanon
stories.description: Israel destroys solar panels in south Lebanon

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 58 as Tehran-Washington talks stall?
stories.description: Trump calls off planned trip to Pakistan by his envoys, in the latest setback to efforts to end the war with Iran.

stories.title: Hundreds of Israelis rally as US-Iran peace talks stall
stories.description: Crowds of protesters took to the streets of Tel Aviv on Sunday, amid fears the US-Israeli war on Iran will resume.

stories.title: Trump safe after shooting at White House correspondents' dinner; suspect in custody
stories.description: United States President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump were rushed out of the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner by Secret Service agents on Saturday night after a man opened fire on security personnel, officials said. A man armed with a shotgun fired at a ...

stories.title: Japan deploys 1,400 firefighters to battle raging wildfires in the north
stories.description: Japan has deployed 1,400 firefighters and 100 Self-Defence Force personnel to battle mountain blazes in ​the northern part of the country, with the fires, ‌now burning on Sunday for a fifth straight day, continuing to threaten a picturesque coastal town. The area consumed by the ...

stories.title: Six killed in Israeli strikes on Lebanon
stories.description: BEIRUT: Lebanon’s health ministry said Israeli strikes on Saturday in the country’s south killed six people, despite a ceasefire that was extended this week in the war between Israel and Hezbollah. “Two Israeli enemy strikes, on a truck and a motorbike, in the town of Yohmor al-S...

stories.title: Hormuz stays flashpoint amid reopening move
stories.description: • Iran asks US to lift blockade, warns of response if ‘piracy’ continues• Macron wants full reopening of strait in ‘coming days and weeks’• Turkiye, Germany agree to de-mining missions TEHRAN/ANKARA: As a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz by the US and Iran led to energy shortages...

stories.title: Russia pounds Ukraine’s city for 20 hours straight
stories.description: KYIV: Relentless Russian barrages of drones and missiles pounded on Ukraine’s central-eastern city of Dnipro for 20 hours straight, killing six people and wounding dozens, local authorities said on Saturday. The attack — the largest ever on the city — began overnight and lasted w...

stories.title: US State Department orders global warning about alleged AI theft by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
stories.description: The US State Department has ordered a global push to bring attention to what it says are widespread efforts by Chinese companies, including AI startup DeepSeek, to steal intellectual property from US artificial intelligence labs, according to a diplomatic cable seen by Reuters. T...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war live: Oman's Sultan meets Iran's Araghchi to discuss regional situation
stories.description: U.S. President Trump cancels Pakistan talks trip and says Iran war on hold; U.S. Navy intercepts Iran-linked vessel in Arabian Sea

stories.title: Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf | Iran’s wartime apparatchik
stories.description: In a country caught between external attacks and internal pressure, with much of its top leadership killed by its enemies, the Parliament Speaker, who cut his teeth in the 1980-88 Iran-Iraq war and served the IRGC as a commander, emerged as the new face of the revolutionary syste...

stories.title: Pakistan PM dials Iranian President, discusses evolving regional situation
stories.description: During their warm and cordial conversation that lasted fifty minutes, the two leaders had a detailed exchange of views on the current regional situation and ongoing efforts to promote peace and stability in the region, says the Pakistan Prime Minister's office.

stories.title: U.S. Navy intercepts Iran-linked vessel in Arabian Sea
stories.description: The U.S. Department of Treasury sanctioned M/V Sevan and 18 other “shadow fleet” vessels for transporting billions in Iranian energy products to foreign markets

stories.title: Trump says cancelled envoys' Pakistan trip, but Iran war not resuming
stories.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said Mr. Araghchi had left Islamabad and arrived in Muscat on Saturday (April 25) for meetings with Omani officials

stories.title: Donald Trump says shooting won’t ‘deter’ him from Iran war
stories.description: The U.S. President had cancelled his envoys’ trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran

stories.title: Top US diplomat avoids participating in Iran talks — newspaper
stories.description: The Financial Times emphasized that Secretary of State Marco Rubio was absent during both of the US delegation's trips to Islamabad

stories.title: Iran says US must stop threats for talks to resume — newspaper
stories.description: Sources told The Wall Street Journal that if Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi deems a deal with Washington possible, the parties could meet in the coming days

stories.title: US actions against Iran undermine trust in Washington — president
stories.description: Masoud Pezeshkian also expressed concern that the negotiations "could be a smokescreen" for a new attack on Iran

stories.title: US redirects 37 vessels since start of naval blockade of Iran — Pentagon
stories.description: CENTCOM reports that the servicemen "continue to enforce US sanctions"
